scanSection.title: Library Scan
scanSection.description: Scan movie folders and video files (uses ffprobe on video files to refresh quality metadata). By default, already scanned files are skipped. Enable "Force rescan all files" below to re-scan all files.
scanSection.libraryPathLabel: Library Path (leave blank to use saved Movies roots)
scanSection.rescanChecked: Checked: full rescan (slower, runs ffprobe again on all video files).
scanSection.rescanUnchecked: Unchecked: incremental scan (faster, skips already scanned files).

syncSection.title: Jellyfin Sync
syncSection.description: Fetch curation metadata from Jellyfin (ratings, genres, IDs) and enrich the local database. Curatarr does not mirror full metadata fields like overview because Jellyfin remains the source of truth.
syncSection.guide:
Jellyfin Sync - how it works

1. Fetches your entire movie library from Jellyfin in pages (100 items per batch).

2. For each Jellyfin item, tries to find the matching local folder using 5 strategies (in order):
   - Exact folder path match   -> confidence 1.0
   - IMDb ID match             -> confidence 1.0
   - Normalised title + year   -> confidence 0.95
   - Normalised title only     -> confidence 0.75 (flags year diff)
   - Fuzzy title (>= 85% sim)  -> confidence proportional

3. Matched movies are enriched with curation metadata needed for Curatarr decisions:
   ratings, genres, IMDb/TMDb IDs.
   Full metadata (including overview and long-form descriptions) remains in Jellyfin.

4. Ambiguous matches (year mismatch, fuzzy title) are flagged
   and visible on the Disambiguate page for manual review.

5. Results stream live over SSE - progress is visible in the
   modal that opens when you click "Sync from Jellyfin".

A full sync of 1 000+ movies typically takes 20-60 seconds,
depending on Jellyfin's response time.

history.title: Scan History
history.noChanges: No changes detected - all files already scanned
